metadata.name: argus
metadata.description: VESTA transfer-hook policy engine: aegis-verified eligibility + velocity/list controls

instructions.name: accept_guard_authority
docs: Accept a proposed guard authority (two-step, spec §3.4).

instructions.name: accept_protocol_authority
docs: Accept a proposed protocol authority (two-step).

instructions.name: activate_policy
docs: Activate an approved version after the timelock (Activator role).

instructions.name: add_list_entry
docs: Add an allow/deny list member — guard authority only (spec §3.5).

instructions.name: anchor_statement
docs: Anchor a period's decision-statement Merkle root on-chain (Reporter role,
spec 10 phase 2) — tamper-evident and provably complete.

accounts.name: license
docs: Statements are a premium feature — a live license with the STATEMENTS
entitlement is required (spec 10 §4.7, the revenue wedge).

instructions.name: apply_role_change
docs: Apply a queued role reassignment after its timelock (RoleAdmin).

instructions.name: approve_policy
docs: Approve a pending version and start the timelock (Approver ≠ Author).

instructions.name: bump_screening_epoch
docs: Advance the screening epoch (spec 10 phase 4 SANCTIONS): instantly stale
every cached capability of the mint for near-real-time freeze propagation.

instructions.name: configure_policy
docs: Retune the per-mint policy — guard authority only (spec §3.2).

instructions.name: execute
docs: Invoked by Token-2022 on every transfer of a hooked mint (spec §5).

accounts.name: guard_config
docs: Per-mint policy (spec §2.1). Deserialization failure fails closed.

accounts.name: capability
docs: argus; read only on the REQUIRE_ATTESTATION path (spec 09) — no aegis CPI.

instructions.name: initialize_governance
docs: Adopt the governed policy lifecycle for a mint (guard authority only).
Seeds the role registry, active-policy pointer, and a genesis version
capturing the current config; thereafter `configure_policy` is disabled
and all changes run propose → approve → timelock → activate.

accounts.name: genesis_version
docs: The genesis version — captures the current `GuardConfig` as the first
immutable, content-addressed policy (already active, no timelock).

instructions.name: initialize_protocol
docs: Trust-on-first-use creation of the protocol config + fee treasury.

instructions.name: initialize_transfer_guard
docs: Permissioned creation of the GuardConfig + ExtraAccountMetaList,
seeded with an initial policy (spec §3.1).

accounts.name: merchant
docs: owner program, discriminator, PDA derivation, and field bindings.

accounts.name: guard_config
docs: Per-mint policy account (spec §2.1).

accounts.name: extra_account_meta_list
docs: TLV-initialized in the handler (defensively — the address is predictable).

instructions.name: invalidate_capability
docs: Guard-authority: immediately invalidate a subject's cached capability
(e.g. on a known aegis-side revocation), without a global epoch bump.

instructions.name: open_wallet_state
docs: One-time, customer-signed velocity-state creation — in-hook creation is
impossible (privilege de-escalation leaves no rent payer, spec §3.6).

instructions.name: pin_policy
docs: Finalize the active version as immutable — freeze-only stays alive
(RoleAdmin). Configurable immutability, not an all-or-nothing cliff.

instructions.name: propose_policy
docs: Propose an immutable, content-addressed policy version (Author role).

instructions.name: propose_role_change
docs: Queue a timelocked role reassignment (RoleAdmin).

instructions.name: purchase_license
docs: Tenant pays the fee to extend their license by `periods` (spec 10 §4.7).

accounts.name: authority
docs: The tenant's guard authority pays and signs.

instructions.name: refresh_eligibility
docs: Off-hot-path: cache a subject's aegis eligibility as a capability that
`execute` reads with no CPI (spec 09). Permissionless; bundle before a
transfer when the capability is missing or stale.

accounts.name: attestation
docs: through to aegis `verify`/`verify_policy`, which re-derive and owner-check
it; a wrong or missing account yields a negative verdict (fail safe).

accounts.name: policy
docs: one (`guard_config.policy`). Ignored on the legacy `Present` path.

instructions.name: remove_list_entry
docs: Remove an allow/deny list member — guard authority only (spec §3.5).

instructions.name: reverify_accreditation
docs: Permissionless crank: re-check the governing issuer's aegis accreditation
and auto-degrade (after grace) or auto-restore the transfer posture.

accounts.name: cranker
docs: Anyone may crank — the check is deterministic and mutates only health
state; there is no privileged input to abuse.

accounts.name: aegis_trust_root
docs: and owner-checks it; a wrong account yields a negative verdict (fail safe).

instructions.name: rollback_policy
docs: Expedited re-point to any prior approved version (Activator role).

instructions.name: set_degrade_mode
docs: Guard-authority manual posture override — emergency degrade, or restore
to NORMAL after resolving a dispute (challenge path).

instructions.name: set_guard_paused
docs: Per-mint peer-transfer circuit breaker — guard authority only (spec §3.3).

instructions.name: set_license
docs: Grant/update a mint's premium license terms (protocol authority).

instructions.name: set_license_fee
docs: Set the per-period license fee (protocol authority).

instructions.name: set_trust_anchor
docs: Bind the mint's governing issuer to an aegis accreditation root (guard
authority). Configures the fall-to posture and grace window.

instructions.name: transfer_guard_authority
docs: Propose a new guard authority (two-step, spec §3.4).

instructions.name: transfer_protocol_authority
docs: Propose a new protocol authority (two-step).

instructions.name: withdraw_fees
docs: Withdraw accrued license fees from the treasury (protocol authority).

errors.name: UnauthorizedGuardInit
errors.msg: Guard initialization not authorized for this merchant/mint

errors.name: GuardAlreadyInitialized
errors.msg: Transfer guard already initialized for this mint

errors.name: MintMismatch
errors.msg: Mint does not match the merchant point mint

errors.name: StateNotOpened
errors.msg: Wallet policy state not opened — run open_wallet_state first

errors.name: Unauthorized
errors.msg: Not the guard authority

errors.name: PendingAuthorityMismatch
errors.msg: Pending authority does not match the signer

errors.name: InvalidPolicy
errors.msg: Policy values are inconsistent (e.g. per-tx cap above daily cap)

errors.name: UnknownFlag
errors.msg: Unknown policy flag bit set

errors.name: MintPaused
errors.msg: Per-mint transfers are paused

errors.name: GiftingDisabled
errors.msg: Peer gifting is disabled for this mint

errors.name: PerTxExceeded
errors.msg: Per-transfer cap exceeded

errors.name: BalanceCapExceeded
errors.msg: Destination balance cap would be exceeded

errors.name: CooldownActive
errors.msg: Transfer cooldown has not elapsed

errors.name: TransferCountExceeded
errors.msg: Daily transfer count cap reached

errors.name: GiftCapExceeded
errors.msg: Daily gift cap exceeded

errors.name: NotAllowlisted
errors.msg: Destination is not in the allow list

errors.name: DenyListed
errors.msg: Destination is in the deny list

errors.name: AttestationFailed
errors.msg: Attestation missing, expired, revoked, or does not satisfy policy

errors.name: ProgramOwnedDestination
errors.msg: Destination wallet is program-owned — not a loyalty flow

errors.name: MetaListMismatch
errors.msg: Provided extra account does not match the meta list derivation

errors.name: NotTransferring
errors.msg: execute invoked outside a genuine Token-2022 transfer

errors.name: EligibilityStale
errors.msg: Eligibility capability missing or stale — run refresh_eligibility

errors.name: AegisProgramMismatch
errors.msg: aegis program does not match the guard's configured aegis deployment

errors.name: UnsupportedVersion
errors.msg: Account layout version is not supported

errors.name: Overflow
errors.msg: Arithmetic overflow

errors.name: RoleUnauthorized
errors.msg: Signer does not hold the required governance role

errors.name: InvalidRole
errors.msg: Unknown governance role

errors.name: SelfApproval
errors.msg: Approver must differ from the version author (separation of duties)

errors.name: PolicyNotApproved
errors.msg: Policy version is not approved

errors.name: TimelockActive
errors.msg: Governance timelock has not elapsed

errors.name: PolicyPinned
errors.msg: Policy is pinned — no further changes permitted

errors.name: PolicyVersionMismatch
errors.msg: Provided policy version does not match the pointer state

errors.name: PolicyHashMismatch
errors.msg: Provided policy version hash does not match its content

errors.name: NoPendingChange
errors.msg: No pending governance change is queued

errors.name: InvalidTimelock
errors.msg: Timelock value is out of the permitted range

errors.name: TrustDegraded
errors.msg: Peer transfers are blocked by the trust-triangle degrade posture

errors.name: InvalidDegradeTarget
errors.msg: Degrade target must be a real degraded posture

errors.name: TrustAnchorMissing
errors.msg: Trust anchor is not configured for this mint

errors.name: LicenseNotEntitled
errors.msg: License does not grant the required entitlement or has expired

errors.name: InvalidEntitlement
errors.msg: Entitlement bits are out of the permitted range

errors.name: InsufficientFee
errors.msg: Insufficient funds to cover the license fee

fields.name: degrade_mode
docs: Live posture after this crank.

types.name: ArgusProtocol
docs: Protocol-wide config + fee treasury (spec 10 §4.7), seeds `["protocol"]`.
The PDA itself is the fee vault — lamports from `purchase_license` accrue on
it and the protocol authority withdraws them. Trust-on-first-use init, then
two-step authority handover (no god key retained).

fields.name: license_fee_lamports
docs: Lamports charged per `purchase_license` period.

types.name: CapabilityInvalidated
docs: A subject's cached capability was force-invalidated by the guard authority.

fields.name: automatic
docs: True when set by the permissionless crank; false for a manual override.

types.name: EligibilityCapability
docs: Cached eligibility verdict for a subject (spec 09 §4.1). `refresh_eligibility`
pays aegis's `verify` CPI once, off the transfer path, and stamps the result
here; `execute` then does an O(1) freshness + bitmap read with NO CPI. A
missing/stale capability fails the transfer closed (client must refresh).

fields.name: verdicts
docs: Bit `i` set = predicate `i` satisfied (see `crate::constants` bits).

fields.name: aegis_program
docs: aegis deployment consulted when this capability was minted.

fields.name: policy_epoch
docs: `GuardConfig.policy_epoch` at mint time; must still match to be valid.

fields.name: screening_epoch
docs: `GuardConfig.screening_epoch` at mint time (spec 10 §4.4 SANCTIONS). A
screening-epoch bump invalidates every capability of the mint regardless
of TTL — near-real-time sanctions/freeze propagation with no polling.

fields.name: jurisdiction
docs: Accreditation provenance carried from the aegis verdict (spec 10 §4.4
CORRIDOR): the issuer's jurisdiction and tier at verification time.

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Unix expiry; the capability is stale once `now >= expires_at`.

types.name: EligibilityRefreshed
docs: A subject's eligibility was (re)computed via aegis `verify` and cached.

types.name: GuardConfig
docs: Per-mint transfer policy — the tunable heart of the guard (spec §2.1).
One account, read once in `execute`. The guard authority (the merchant PDA)
retunes fields via `configure_policy`; the trusted aegis issuer/program are
bound at init. Eligibility is no longer read from aegis by byte offset —
`execute` reads a cached `EligibilityCapability` (spec 09) minted off the hot
path by `refresh_eligibility`, which consumes aegis's `verify` interface.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version (Track B convention).

fields.name: mint
docs: The hooked mint this config governs.

fields.name: authority
docs: Who may retune policy — the vesta_core Merchant PDA (rotatable two-step).

fields.name: pending_authority
docs: Two-step authority rotation target.

fields.name: treasury
docs: Always-allowed destination (rule 2): the merchant treasury ATA.

fields.name: aegis_program
docs: aegis deployment this guard trusts (the `verify` program).

fields.name: attestation_issuer
docs: aegis issuer whose credentials this guard trusts (spec §7).

fields.name: policy
docs: aegis `Policy` this guard enforces (spec 07). When set, `refresh_eligibility`
consumes `verify_policy` — so the compliance rule (jurisdiction, schema,
freshness) lives in aegis as data, editable with NO argus redeploy. When
`default()`, the legacy single-credential `Present` check is used instead.

fields.name: policy_epoch
docs: Bumped on any policy change; stamped into capabilities so a config change
invalidates stale eligibility regardless of TTL (spec 09 §4.4).

fields.name: screening_epoch
docs: Fast-revocation clock (spec 10 §4.4 SANCTIONS). Bumped by
`bump_screening_epoch` on a screening/sanctions event; a capability whose
`screening_epoch` differs is rejected regardless of TTL, so a freeze
propagates on the next transfer without waiting for expiry.

fields.name: paused
docs: Per-mint peer-transfer freeze (rules 1–2 stay open — clawback/refunds).

fields.name: flags
docs: Policy bitset — see `crate::constants::flags`.

fields.name: daily_gift_cap
docs: Raw units a wallet may send per UTC day (0 = gifting off).

fields.name: per_tx_cap
docs: Max raw units in a single peer transfer (0 = no per-tx limit).

fields.name: max_wallet_balance
docs: Reject transfers that would push the destination over this (0 = off).

fields.name: transfers_per_day_cap
docs: Max peer transfers a wallet may make per UTC day (0 = no count limit).

fields.name: cooldown_secs
docs: Minimum seconds between a wallet's peer transfers (0 = no cooldown).

fields.name: attestation_schema
docs: Required aegis schema id (meaningful only with REQUIRE_ATTESTATION).

fields.name: capability_ttl_secs
docs: Per-guard eligibility-capability TTL, seconds (0 = protocol default). A
strict mint sets this low to shrink the aegis-revocation-latency window;
`invalidate_capability` closes it immediately for a known revocation.

fields.name: governed
docs: True once the mint adopts the governed policy lifecycle (spec 10 §4.1).
While set, `configure_policy` (free-tier live mutation) is rejected — all
policy changes must go through propose → approve → timelock → activate.

fields.name: active_policy_hash
docs: Hash of the currently-active governed `PolicyVersion` (`[0;32]` for a
free-tier mint). Denormalized here so `execute` can stamp every decision
record with the exact deciding policy at zero hot-path cost (spec 10 §4.5).

fields.name: degrade_mode
docs: Live trust-triangle posture (spec 10 §4.3), driven by the off-path
`reverify_accreditation` crank. `NORMAL` = flow per policy; any degraded
mode blocks peer gifts while keeping redemption/clawback open (never
strands assets). Denormalized so `execute` enforces it with no extra load.

types.name: InitialPolicy
docs: Full policy supplied at guard init. The aegis program + issuer are fixed
here for the life of the guard; everything else is retunable via
`configure_policy`.

fields.name: aegis_program
docs: aegis deployment to consult (`verify`); default to the canonical AEGIS_ID.

fields.name: policy
docs: aegis `Policy` to enforce (jurisdiction/schema/freshness as data). When
`default()`, the legacy `Present` check over (issuer, schema) is used.

fields.name: attestation_issuer
docs: aegis issuer to trust; `Pubkey::default()` when attestation is unused.

fields.name: capability_ttl_secs
docs: Eligibility-capability TTL, seconds (0 = protocol default). Lower =
tighter aegis-revocation latency.

types.name: LicenseState
docs: Per-mint premium license (spec 10 §4.7), seeds `["license", mint]`. Governs
access to premium features (governance/statements/trust/screening) via an
entitlement bitmap + expiry. Absent or expired ⇒ the mint runs at the free
tier — premium ops are refused but transfers/redemption never stop, so a
business-reason expiry can never strand holder assets.

fields.name: expires_at
docs: Unix expiry (`0` = perpetual).

fields.name: rollback
docs: True when this activation was an expedited rollback to a prior version.

types.name: PolicyDoc
docs: The tunable policy fields under governance — the content that a
`PolicyVersion` hashes and that `activate_policy` writes onto `GuardConfig`
(spec 10 §4.1). Deliberately the retunable subset: the aegis *program* and
trusted issuer are bound immutably at guard init and are NOT governed here.

fields.name: policy
docs: aegis `Policy` to enforce (`Pubkey::default()` = legacy `Present` check).

types.name: PolicyListEntry
docs: Allow/deny list membership marker (spec §2.4). Existence under the argus
program == membership; the account carries no payload beyond identity.

types.name: PolicyPointer
docs: Per-mint active-policy pointer + pending-change timelock (spec 10 §4.1, §5).
This is the governed replacement for silent live-mutation of `GuardConfig`.

fields.name: active_hash
docs: Hash of the currently-active `PolicyVersion`.

fields.name: pending_hash
docs: Hash of a proposed version awaiting approval/timelock (`[0;32]` = none).

fields.name: pending_approved_at
docs: Approval ts of the pending version (`0` = proposed-but-unapproved).

fields.name: timelock_secs
docs: Seconds that must elapse after approval before activation.

fields.name: pinned
docs: Configurable immutability (spec 10 §4.1): once pinned, no propose/activate;
only the freeze (pause) authority stays alive.

types.name: PolicyUpdate
docs: Partial retune. Every field is optional; `None` leaves the value untouched.
The aegis program/issuer are intentionally absent — immutable post-init.

types.name: PolicyVersion
docs: An immutable, content-addressed proposed/approved policy (spec 10 §4.1).
Seeds `["pver", mint, policy_hash]` — the hash IS the identity, so a version
can never be silently rewritten. Lifecycle metadata (author/approver/times)
is appended; the `doc` itself is frozen at propose time.

fields.name: policy_hash
docs: sha256 of `doc` — equals the PDA seed component.

fields.name: author
docs: Role::Author key that proposed it.

fields.name: approver
docs: Role::Approver key that approved it (`default()` until approved).

fields.name: approved_at
docs: Unix ts of approval (`0` until approved) — the timelock reference.

types.name: RoleAssignment
docs: All six role authorities assigned when a mint adopts governance. Any key may
be a multisig (e.g. a Squads PDA); argus treats it as one signer. Author and
Approver SHOULD be distinct — approval enforces `approver != author`, so a
mint that sets them equal can never activate a change.

types.name: RoleRegistry
docs: Per-mint role registry (spec 10 §4.2), seeds `["roles", mint]`. A mint that
never adopts governance never creates this; free-tier stays single-authority.

fields.name: pending_role
docs: A two-step, timelocked pending role change (spec 10 §4.2 "timelocked").
`pending_after == 0` means no change is queued.

types.name: ScreeningEpochBumped
docs: The screening epoch was advanced — every cached capability of the mint is
now stale (spec 10 §4.4 SANCTIONS fast-freeze).

types.name: StatementAnchored
docs: A period's decision statement was anchored on-chain (spec 10 §4.5).

types.name: StatementCommitment
docs: Tamper-evident anchor for a period's decision records (spec 10 §4.5), seeds
`["statement", mint, period_le]`. An off-chain indexer materializes the
canonical, reason-coded decisions for the period into a Merkle tree; the
Reporter role anchors its root here. `decision_count` makes the statement
**provably complete** — an omission changes the count and the root.

fields.name: period
docs: Period identifier (e.g. a UTC-day index) — also the PDA seed component.

fields.name: decision_count
docs: Number of decisions covered — completeness witness.

types.name: TransferDecision
docs: One event per `execute` call, carrying a stable canonical reason code
(`crate::constants::reason`) plus the exact deciding policy (`policy_epoch` +
`active_policy_hash`, spec 10 §4.5). The complete allow/deny audit trail —
an indexer folds these into period `StatementCommitment` roots.

fields.name: policy_epoch
docs: Monotonic policy epoch in force at decision time.

fields.name: active_policy_hash
docs: Hash of the active governed policy (`[0;32]` for a free-tier mint).

types.name: TrustAnchor
docs: The trust triangle (spec 10 §4.3), seeds `["trust", mint]`. Binds the mint's
governing issuer to an aegis accreditation root: the permissionless
`reverify_accreditation` crank re-checks the chain and, after a grace window,
trips `GuardConfig.degrade_mode` to `degrade_target` — so a revoked issuer's
authority evaporates without any human key. Recovery auto-restores on the
next healthy crank.

fields.name: accreditation_root
docs: aegis accreditation root the governing issuer must chain to.

fields.name: subject_issuer
docs: The aegis issuer identity being continuously verified.

fields.name: required_schema
docs: Required accreditation schema ("accreditation type").

fields.name: aegis_program
docs: aegis deployment (must equal `GuardConfig.aegis_program`).

fields.name: degrade_target
docs: Posture to fall to on sustained failure (`REDEMPTION_ONLY` / `FROZEN`).

fields.name: grace_secs
docs: Seconds a failing streak must persist before auto-degrade bites — the
tolerance for an aegis outage / transient false-negative (spec 10 §7).

fields.name: failing_since
docs: Unix ts the current failing streak began (`0` = healthy).

fields.name: last_verified_at
docs: Unix ts of the last successful reverify.

types.name: WalletPolicyState
docs: Per-(mint, source-owner) velocity counters (spec §2.2). Deliberately
non-closable: closing and reopening would reset the daily counters, so the
locked rent is the anti-reset bond.

fields.name: day
docs: UTC day the counters below belong to.

fields.name: sent_today
docs: Raw units sent as peer transfers today.

fields.name: transfers_today
docs: Peer-transfer count today.

fields.name: last_transfer_at
docs: Unix ts of the wallet's last peer transfer (cooldown reference).

constants.name: CAP_SEED
docs: Per-(mint, subject) cached eligibility verdict (spec 09 §4.1).

constants.name: DEFAULT_DAILY_GIFT_CAP_RAW
docs: Default daily gift velocity cap seeded at guard init, raw units
(= 500.00 pts at issue). Retunable via `configure_policy` thereafter.

constants.name: EXTRA_ACCOUNT_METAS_SEED
docs: Interface-mandated seed for the ExtraAccountMetaList PDA.

constants.name: GUARD_SEED
docs: Per-mint policy account (spec §2.1).

constants.name: LICENSE_SEED
docs: Per-mint premium license (spec 10 §4.7): `["license", mint]`.

constants.name: LIST_ENTRY_SEED
docs: Allow/deny list membership marker (spec §2.4).

constants.name: POLICY_POINTER_SEED
docs: Per-mint active-policy pointer + timelock state (spec 10 §5): `["active", mint]`.

constants.name: POLICY_VERSION_SEED
docs: Immutable, content-addressed policy version (spec 10 §5): `["pver", mint, hash]`.

constants.name: PROTOCOL_SEED
docs: Protocol-wide config + fee treasury singleton (spec 10 §4.7): `["protocol"]`.

constants.name: ROLES_SEED
docs: Per-mint separation-of-duties role registry (spec 10 §5): `["roles", mint]`.

constants.name: STATEMENT_SEED
docs: Per-(mint, period) decision-statement anchor (spec 10 §5): `["statement", mint, period]`.

constants.name: TRUST_SEED
docs: Per-mint trust-triangle anchor (spec 10 §5): `["trust", mint]`.

constants.name: WALLET_STATE_SEED
docs: Per-(mint, source-owner) velocity counters (spec §2.2).
